get_current_time.description: Returns the current date and time in Oslo local time (Europe/Oslo) format "2026-05-16T23:59:59". Use this to determine "now" before creating time-based records (sleep start, medicine dose, etc.).

get_mcp_build_time.description: Returns the build timestamp of the MCP server. Use this when the user asks about the MCP server version or build time.

get_drank_milk.description: List all drank milk records. Shows how much milk the baby consumed, from which source (FRIDGE/BOOB). Optionally filter by date range.

params.from: Oslo local datetime (e.g. "2026-05-16T00:00:00") — filter records created on or after this time
params.to: Oslo local datetime (e.g. "2026-05-16T23:59:59") — filter records created on or before this time

create_drank_milk.description: Record that the baby drank milk. Source is FRIDGE (bottle — default) or BOOB. isNewBottle controls whether a new record is created (true) or added to the latest existing record (false).

params.amount: Amount in ml the baby drank
params.source: Where the milk came from. Defaults to FRIDGE (bottle).
params.isNewBottle: true = create a new record; false = add amount to the latest existing record

log_milk_waste.description: Subtract wasted milk from the latest drank milk record (FRIDGE only). Does NOT restore milk to storage. The amount is floored at 0.

params.amount: Amount in ml that was wasted

get_sleep.description: List all sleep records. Each record has a start time and an optional end time (null means baby is still sleeping). Optionally filter by date range.

params.from: Oslo local datetime (e.g. "2026-05-16T00:00:00") — filter records created on or after this time
params.to: Oslo local datetime (e.g. "2026-05-16T23:59:59") — filter records created on or before this time

get_latest_sleep.description: Get the most recent sleep record. Useful to check if the baby is currently sleeping (end = null means still asleep). Returns null if no sleep records exist.

start_sleep.description: Record that the baby fell asleep now. Creates a new sleep record with start = now and end = null.

end_sleep.description: Record that the baby woke up. Sets the end time on the most recent open sleep record (where end = null).

get_pee.description: List all pee events. Each event is a simple timestamp. Optionally filter by date range.

params.from: Oslo local datetime (e.g. "2026-05-16T00:00:00") — filter events on or after this time
params.to: Oslo local datetime (e.g. "2026-05-16T23:59:59") — filter events on or before this time

log_pee.description: Log a new pee event. No body required — timestamp is set to now.

get_poop.description: List all poop events. Each event is a simple timestamp. Optionally filter by date range.

params.from: Oslo local datetime (e.g. "2026-05-16T00:00:00") — filter events on or after this time
params.to: Oslo local datetime (e.g. "2026-05-16T23:59:59") — filter events on or before this time

log_poop.description: Log a new poop event. No body required — timestamp is set to now.

get_medicines.description: List all active medicines with their latest dose timestamp. Use this to discover medicine IDs before logging a dose. Shows which medicines exist and when they were last given.

log_medicine_dose.description: Record that a dose of a specific medicine was given. Call get_medicines first to find the correct medicine ID. Defaults to now if takenAt is not provided.

params.takenAt: Oslo local datetime (e.g. "2026-05-16T08:00:00") when dose was taken (defaults to now)

get_pumping.description: List all pumping (breast pump) entries. Optionally filter by date range.

params.from: Oslo local datetime (e.g. "2026-05-16T00:00:00") — filter entries on or after this time
params.to: Oslo local datetime (e.g. "2026-05-16T23:59:59") — filter entries on or before this time

get_latest_pumping.description: Get the most recent pumping entry. Returns null if no entries exist.

log_pumping.description: Log a new pumping event. Timestamp is set to now.

get_all_data.description: Get a full dump of ALL data from every table (served_milk, drank_milk, sleep, pee, poop, medicine, medicine_log, pumping). Use this for broad analysis across all categories. Returns a large JSON object keyed by table name.
